Block.Para: Third Session, Fortieth Parliament,
59 Elizabeth II, 2010
HOUSE OF COMMONS OF CANADA
BILL C-465
An Act respecting a National Hunting, Trapping and Fishing Heritage Day
Reprinted as amended by the Standing Committee on Canadian Heritage as a working copy for the use of the House of Commons at Report Stage and as reported to the House on October脗聽20, 2010

Also available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca
SUMMARY
This enactment designates the third Saturday in September in each and every year as 芒聙聹National Hunting, Trapping and Fishing Heritage Day芒聙聺.

Bill_Part.RunningHead_E: National Hunting, Trapping and Fishing Heritage Day

Block.Para: 3rd Session, 40th Parliament,
59 Elizabeth II, 2010
house of commons of canada
BILL C-465
An Act respecting a National Hunting, Trapping and Fishing Heritage Day
Whereas hunting, trapping and fishing are part of Canada芒聙聶s national heritage;

Para.change: Whereas the Aboriginal peoples of Canada have practiced and been sustained by traditional hunting, trapping and fishing activities for food and for ceremonial and commercial purposes since time immemorial;

Block.Para: Whereas Canada芒聙聶s hunters, trappers and fishers have made a significant contribution to the development of our nation by traversing and mapping the prairies, forests, streams and rivers from coast to coast to coast;
Whereas millions of Canadians participate in and enjoy hunting, trapping and fishing;
And whereas hunting, trapping and fishing contribute significantly to the national economy;
Now, therefore, Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
SHORT TITLE
1. This Act may be cited as the National Hunting, Trapping and Fishing Heritage Day Act.
NATIONAL HUNTING, TRAPPING AND FISHING HERITAGE DAY

Block.MarginalNote: National Hunting, Trapping and Fishing Heritage Day
Block.Para: 2. Throughout Canada, in each and every year, the third Saturday in September shall be known as 芒聙聹National Hunting, Trapping and Fishing Heritage Day芒聙聺.
3. For greater certainty, National Hunting, Trapping and Fishing Heritage Day is not a legal holiday or a non-juridical day.
Published under authority of the Speaker of the House of Commons
脗聞
